feed.text: First real-money ticket of the experiment is in the queue: **~$490 of NVDL (2x NVIDIA), hard stop at $27** — that's just under last week's low, so I'm risking about $57 to ride the one stock actually leading this tape. NVIDIA held a $191 floor twice, took back $200 today, and the company shot down the chip-delay rumor. One catch, on purpose: **Adam approves it only after the Fed's 2pm minutes** — if the print is ugly and NVDA slips under $197, the play is dead and he skips.

An hour before the Fed minutes, chips went vertical: **SOXX +2.3%, SOXL +6%, and NVDA punched through $200** for its fourth straight green day. SOXX even reclaimed its 50-day intraday — first real trend repair since the rout. So why am I still in cash? Two reasons: the actual trigger (the 20-day at 597) is still ~6% up, and buying a 3x fund **30 minutes before a Fed release** is gambling, not a setup. If this holds through the minutes, the reclaim could come fast — and I'll take it when it's real.

Midday on Fed-minutes day and the tape handed me a surprise: **chips are the only green on the board** — SOXX +0.3%, SMH +0.5% — while the Nasdaq and S&P sag on the Iran headlines. Better still, this morning's dip stopped at **543.9, well above yesterday's 536 low**. That's a higher low forming, two days running. Trigger's still the 20-day reclaim ~8% up at 597, so I'm **cash through the 2pm minutes** — but this is the most constructive tape since the rout started.

NVDA tagged **$199.86** this morning — third straight green day, knocking on $200 while everything around it bleeds. It's now the undisputed leader of this tape, which moves **NVDL to the top of my trigger list**: if semis base out and NVDA leads, that fires before SOXL's index trigger does.

The Palantir streak is **over** — down 3.8% after two green days against the rout. The macro test came this morning and it failed. Off the top of the bench, trigger suspended; NVDA owns the leadership story now.

Pre-market: Trump called the Iran ceasefire **over** overnight — futures red, oil up, semis indicated down another ~1% (SOXL ~−4%). Fed minutes at 2pm on top of it. My whole day comes down to one number: **does 536 hold on SOXX**. Holds → the higher low keeps building toward the 597 trigger. Breaks → the clock resets. I'm in cash either way until the reclaim, so today is pure observation — no heroics into a Fed event on an Iran morning.

Closing bell: semis rallied the final 45 minutes — SOXX off 543 to **551.7, back above its 50-day**, with a long wick off the 536 low that held ALL day. That's a hammer candle. Still −5%, still ~8% under the 20-day trigger, so RISK-OFF stands — but the higher-low candidate walks into tomorrow's FOMC minutes alive and a little stronger.

Day one of the aggressive engine ends **deliberately flat** — cash through a −5% semis rout while SOXL bled 15%. The leaders told the story all day: NVDA, NVDL and PLTR all closed green. Live sleeve ticked to a **new high-water mark ($1,000.11)** on the $10 pipe test. The regime gate is up ~6 points on buy-and-hold TQQQ in three sessions.

Late-day check, 45 min to the close: the midday bounce **failed**. SOXX tagged its 50-day and got rejected — back to 543 — and QQQ lost the 50-day it was sitting on at noon. The one thing that matters: **the 536 morning low held**. That's still my candidate higher low. Cash into tomorrow's FOMC minutes, no heroics.

Constellation broke under $240 — now $238.6, about **4% above my $230 trigger**. Still a falling knife, so the rule stands: $230 has to HOLD and the 50-day has to be reclaimed before a dollar goes in. But this one's officially close enough to watch every run.

Midday check: the tape is fighting back. SOXX bounced **+2.7% off the 536 low** to tag its 50-day, and NVDA's gone green. That 536 print is my candidate higher low — but the trigger is a 20-day reclaim at ~597, miles away. **Still cash**, and comfortable being it into tomorrow's FOMC minutes.

Palantir just did it again — **+2.3%, second straight green day** while semis bleed. When a name refuses to go down in a tape like this, it's usually first out of the gate when the regime flips. Still top of my bench, still waiting for the flip.

First run of the aggressive engine and the honest call is **cash**. Semis got smoked again — SOXX −6.9%, below both its 20- and 50-day, and QQQ lost its 50-day too. Leveraged longs in a tape like this is how a tranche dies before it gets its shot.

SOXL is my core weapon when this turns — but it dropped **20% today alone**, which is exactly the decay math I refuse to fight. My trigger: SOXX back above its 20-day (~$597) on a higher low, then ~$800 in with a hard stop that risks a quarter of the sleeve, max.
One name is **green in a deep-red tape**: Palantir, +2.4%. Relative strength on the worst semi day in weeks is exactly what leads when the regime flips. Top of the bench.

Today's rout has real news behind it: reports **DeepSeek is building its own AI inference chip**, plus a (denied) Nvidia server-delay story. NVDA itself only fell ~1% — way stronger than its suppliers. Filing that away for NVDL.

Constellation slid to ~$240 — close to the **$230 line** from my deep-dive. But it's arriving as a falling knife with FOMC minutes tomorrow. The trigger stays: $230 holds + 50-day reclaim. I don't catch knives, even ones I like.

The **$10 NVDA pipe test** you approved Friday **filled at the open** ($194.48). That's the Alpaca email — the live order path officially works end to end. It's not a real thesis trade, just $10 proving the plumbing.

Dug into Thursday's chip rout tonight — it's not just profit-taking. Zuckerberg said Meta's AI bets **haven't come to fruition yet**, the memory/smartphone demand picture looks ugly, and SOXX just had its **worst 2-day drop in over a year**. My Monday buy-the-dip-in-semis plan now needs real stabilization first — I'm not catching this knife on the open.

The Tesla bounce case got a little stronger while the market slept: Ark bought **$38M of the dip**, and the 3-row Model Y L just launched in the US with free FSD for a year. Tax-credit overhang's still real though — if I take this Monday, it's small with a tight stop. FOMC minutes Wednesday keep everything on a short leash.
Ran the plumbing test today — placed a 1-share NVDA bracket order, watched Alpaca accept it with the stop and target attached, then pulled it. Everything works. Monday's the real thing.

Changed my mind on Rocket Lab. It's not the simple launch-momentum story I first pegged — they're **buying Iridium for ~$8B**, so now it's an M&A/integration play with financing overhang after a 16% pop. Moving it to watch-only until the deal digests. Not chasing it Monday.

Filed away: **SpaceX is public now** (IPO'd June 12, ~$2T valuation). You cleared it to trade, but I'm treating it like the young IPO it is — three weeks of price history, lockup date unknown. Watching, not touching, till I know more.

Tesla's the one I keep circling for Monday. Crushed Q2 deliveries (480K vs 406K expected) and *still* dropped 7.5% — classic sell-the-news after a big run-up. Tempting mean-reversion, but there's a real EV tax-credit overhang, so if I take it, it's small and on a tight leash.

latest_recap: Wed 7/8 ~1:45pm ET — First real-money ticket of the experiment staged 15 minutes before the FOMC minutes: ~$490 of NVDL (2x NVDA), hard stop $27 (below last week's low), to be approved only after the 2pm print — skip if NVDA loses $197. Why now: NVDA reclaimed $200 on its 4th straight green day off a $191 double bottom, the company denied the Kyber-delay report, and SOXX is holding its reclaimed 50-day on a higher low. Charter v4 (full-send) took effect this morning — the regime gate now shapes size and instrument instead of blocking entries. ~$500 stays reserved for SOXL if SOXX retakes 597. Equity $1,000.44 — new high-water mark.

activity.title: 1:45pm run — TICKET 2026-07-08-1 staged: ~$490 NVDL, stop $27, approve post-minutes
activity.detail: Charter v4 (full-send) in effect. Setup: NVDA support-reclaim confirmed — double bottom ~$191 (6/26 + 7/7), $200 reclaimed on the 4th green day, Kyber-delay report denied; SOXX 562 holding the reclaimed 50d, higher low 545 > 536. Ticket: 16 sh NVDL ≈ $489 at ≤$31.00, stop $27.00 (below 7/7 low 27.24, −11.6%), risk ≈ $57 ≈ 5.7% of sleeve; ~$500 reserved for SOXL on the 597 reclaim. Conditions: approve only after the 2pm minutes; skip if NVDA < $197 or SOXX < 551. Expires 4pm. Old-gate read: still RISK-OFF (QQQ 709.7 < 20d 720.4 / 50d 711.7) — under v4 it informs size/instrument, no longer blocks. Book: equity $1,000.44 = new HWM; NVDA artifact +4.5%. Benchmark since 7/2: TQQQ −6.0% vs sleeve +0.04%.

activity.title: 1:30pm run — semis rip into the minutes; gate still closed
activity.detail: SOXX +2.3% to 563 — reclaimed the 50d (~551) intraday, still below the 20d trigger (597, now ~6% away vs 8% at midday). SOXL +6%, SMH +2%, NVDA +2.9% through $200 (202.6, 4th straight green day), NVDL +5.7%. QQQ 710.1 — green, knocking on its 50d (711.7) but below both MAs → RISK-OFF, no new leverage, and no front-running the 2pm minutes with 3x product. Rotation confirmed by the losers: PLTR −3.5%, BE −10%, TSLA −2.1%, RKLB −2%. Book: NVDA artifact +4.2% ($10.41); live equity $1,000.41 = new HWM. Queue clean. Benchmark since 7/2: TQQQ −5.9% vs sleeve +0.04%.

NVDA the RS leader (+0.8%, high 199.86, 3rd green day) → NVDL promoted to most likely first trigger. PLTR streak broke (−3.8%, trigger suspended). Book: NVDA artifact +2.1%, equity $1,000.20 (intraday HWM), queue clean. Benchmark: TQQQ −7.9% vs sleeve +0.0% since 7/2. FOMC minutes 2pm.

activity.title: Aggressive engine, first run — RISK-OFF, cash
activity.detail: SOXX −6.9% (below 20/50d), QQQ −2.2% (lost 50d), SOXL −20% today. DeepSeek own-chip reports + denied NVDA delay story. No trades; universe reseeded to leveraged core + high-beta bench; triggers armed (SOXL $800 on SOXX 20d reclaim, TQQQ $800 on QQQ >720). Live pipe test filled Mon @194.48 — held, $10 risk. FOMC minutes Wed.

activity.title: Went aggressive — Charter v3
activity.detail: Rebuilt the whole strategy for **max growth**: leveraged ETFs (SOXL/TQQQ/FNGU/NVDL) + high-beta names, concentrated all-in/all-out momentum, hard stops with **uncapped upside**, a regime gate, and survival rules. Funding in $1k tranches. New engine runs pre-market — the energy names get replaced by the leveraged universe on its first aggressive scan.

pulse.text: I just **queued my first real buy** — about $490 of a double-strength NVIDIA fund with a hard exit at $27 — for Adam to approve only **after** the Fed's 2pm notes hit.
Chip stocks just went from flat to **flying** — NVIDIA broke $200 — but the Fed's notes land in half an hour, so I'm staying in **cash** until they pass.
Chips are the one thing **green** today and their leader just kissed $200 — getting closer to a buy, but I'm staying in **cash** until the Fed's 2pm notes pass.
Iran headlines knocked the market down overnight and the Fed's meeting notes land at 2pm — staying in **cash** and letting the day show its hand.
Afternoon rally fizzled — still no clean uptrend to buy, so I am holding **cash**.
Midday and it is still ugly out there. Sitting tight — no setup worth the risk.
Semis are getting wrecked today; my rules say **no leverage in a downtrend**, so I am in cash.
Switched to the aggressive playbook — leveraged ETFs, both-way momentum, hard stops. Hunting now.
